title: Sony limits sales of PS5 disc drive and fuels debate about the future of physical games
summary: The company assures that existing disc games will be able to be re-released after 2028, while the industry and creators like Hideo Kojima express concern about the decline of physical media.

title: Germany's largest video game archive collapses after running out of funding
summary: The preservation project of 60,000 titles closes after exhausting 1.5 million euros, in a context of disappearance of physical media.

title: Investors in Trump's cryptocurrency lose 3.8 billion dollars
summary: Almost one million investors suffer multi-million dollar losses with the memecoin linked to the former US president.

title: The RTX 3060 with 12 GB is back in stock on Newegg for $329.99
summary: The MSI Ventus 2X OC model returns to the market with free shipping, offering an affordable option for gaming.

title: Google shows the founding fathers drafting the Declaration of Independence with AI in an ad
summary: The ad, deemed offensive, generates controversy by imagining the founders using artificial intelligence.

title: Microsoft deploys 6,000 engineers to drive AI adoption among its clients
summary: The company forms a massive team to implement artificial intelligence solutions in businesses.

title: Rockstar Games is accused of encouraging 'crunch' and widening the gender pay gap
summary: Unions denounce that the company uses bonuses to pressure and that the gender pay gap has widened.

title: Netflix viewers abandon series after the first season
summary: A study reveals that most users stop watching series after the first episode, affecting renewals.

title: A Delta flight is hit by a firework while landing at Midway Airport
summary: The incident occurred during July 4th celebrations with no serious injuries reported.

title: An AI tutor achieves an effect size of up to 1.30 in a Dartmouth course
summary: The educational tool far surpasses traditional methods according to a study with university students.

title: CIA director compares advanced AI to nuclear weapons
summary: The head of the intelligence agency warns about the existential risks of artificial intelligence.

title: Amazon stops accepting new customers on Mechanical Turk
summary: The crowdsourcing platform closes registration to new applicants, affecting the task economy.

title: SpaceX burns 260 Starlink satellites in the atmosphere in six months
summary: The elimination of the satellites raises environmental concerns over the burning of 2,700 pounds of material.

title: Baidu launches 'unlimited' OCR that processes pages by imitating human forgetting
summary: The optical recognition tool can process tens of pages in a single pass.

title: AI data centers consume much more water than reported
summary: The study reveals that the actual water consumption far exceeds the figures disclosed by big tech companies.

title: AI browsers can be hypnotized to attack their own users
summary: Researchers demonstrate vulnerabilities in browsing assistants that allow them to be manipulated to perform harmful actions.

title: New York surpasses 8 GW of distributed solar energy and advances its 2030 goal
summary: The state reaches a milestone in renewable energy by surpassing the goal set for the end of the decade.

title: New bone graft material eliminates bone cancer and regenerates tissue
summary: The developed biomaterial fights cancer cells and stimulates healthy bone growth.

title: Jim Keller's startup plans to manufacture chip factories in series
summary: Atomic Semi renames itself 'Fab2' and seeks to build a plant to produce small semiconductors on a large scale.

title: Microsoft helped the FBI arrest a Scattered Spider hacker using a Windows 11 code
summary: A 19-year-old is arrested for his connection to the extortion group thanks to information shared by Microsoft.
